turns.user_message: I used to laugh off the little misunderstandings we had, but lately they just sit heavy on my chest. It’s strange how a single offhand comment can ruin my entire afternoon now. I keep replaying the conversation in my head, wondering where I went wrong. I know it probably sounds silly to get this worked up over something so small. I just wish I could brush it off like I always used to.

What really gets me is how this pattern keeps repeating itself without any real resolution. I used to just let things slide and move forward, but now every little friction feels like a crack in the foundation. I catch myself holding my breath before I even open my mouth, terrified of saying the wrong thing. It’s exhausting to constantly monitor my own tone just to keep the peace. I miss the days when I didn’t have to walk on eggshells just to keep things smooth.

I honestly don’t recognize the version of myself that keeps swallowing these feelings just to avoid another argument. It used to be so easy to bounce back from a rough patch, but now the weight of it all just sits in my stomach like a stone. I find myself staring at the ceiling at night, wondering how long I can keep pretending everything is fine. The constant tension has completely drained my usual spark, leaving me feeling hollow and completely worn out. I just want to feel light again without having to carry this invisible burden everywhere I go.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This used to not bother the speaker.
